{
  "title": "Internal Reasoning Only",
  "subject": "Logic and Critical Thinking",
  "structured_notes": {
    "H1": "Logic Fundamentals",
    "H2": "Types of Reasoning",
    "H3": "Deductive Reasoning",
    "text": "Deductive reasoning is a type of reasoning that involves drawing a conclusion based on a set of premises.\nIt is characterized by its certainty and is often used in mathematics and science.\nHere are some examples of deductive reasoning:\n📌 If all A are B and B are C, then all A are C.\n📌 If it is raining and I am outside, then I will get wet.\n📌 All humans are mortal, and Socrates is a human, therefore Socrates is mortal.\n",
    "H3": "Inductive Reasoning",
    "text": "Inductive reasoning is a type of reasoning that involves making a generalization based on specific observations.\nIt is characterized by its uncertainty and is often used in science and everyday life.\nHere are some examples of inductive reasoning:\n⚠️ Most birds can fly, but there are some species that cannot.\n📌 I have seen many sunrises that are orange, therefore the sun is orange.\n",
    "H3": "Abductive Reasoning",
    "text": "Abductive reasoning is a type of reasoning that involves making an educated guess based on incomplete information.\nIt is often used in science and detective work.\nHere are some examples of abductive reasoning:\n💡 The only way to find the missing person is to search the area.\n📌 The fingerprints on the crime scene match the suspect's fingerprints, therefore the suspect is involved in the crime.\n"
  },

    "Inductive Reasoning": "A type of reasoning that involves making a generalization based on specific observations.",
    "Abductive Reasoning": "A type of reasoning that involves making an educated guess based on incomplete information."
  },
